recommendations.title: Implement ProxyActiveChanged publisher in APIService or Clash API integration
recommendations.detail: ProxyActiveChanged is subscribed by auto_update.go:75 but never published. The proxy-switch must come from APIService.OnProxySwitched callback (core/services/api_service.go) or from wherever proxy selection happens. Either: (1) wire the callback to publish ProxyActiveChanged{GroupTag, NewSelected}, or (2) delete the subscriber if proxy-active notifications are not needed for retry-trigger logic.

recommendations.title: Delete dead event kinds: SubscriptionUpdated, AutoUpdateStatus, PowerResume
recommendations.detail: These three event types are defined but never published or subscribed. SubscriptionUpdated and AutoUpdateStatus are design artifacts (SPEC 047). PowerResume uses legacy callbacks instead (main.go:294). Removing them will shrink events.go and reduce confusion. If auto-update monitoring becomes needed later, the pattern is clear: add new EventKind + payload + Publisher + Subscriber.

recommendations.title: Delete MemoryBus.SubscribeAll() method
recommendations.detail: SubscribeAll exists in the Bus interface and MemoryBus implementation but is never called. Comments state it is for 'logging/debug only' but no production code uses it. Removing it simplifies the interface and reduces cognitive load. If per-event debugging is needed, add a named debug-handler instead or use external event logging.

recommendations.title: Consolidate StateChanged event: make StateService subscribers explicit
recommendations.detail: StateChanged is published by StateService.publishStateChanged() and ui/configurator presenter_save.go, but no subscriber listens via EventBus.Subscribe(). If UI needs to react to state changes, wire explicit subscribers in ui/app.go or the Configurator tab. Otherwise, this is a half-finished feature: either complete it or delete it. The event payload Comments mention future state.Diff typing (SPEC 045 phase 3.2) — clarify if this is on the roadmap.

recommendations.title: Unify power-resume handling via EventBus.Publish(PowerResume)
recommendations.detail: Currently main.go:294 wires platform.RegisterPowerResumeCallback() directly to RefreshAPIFunc + auto-ping logic. Instead: have platform/power_*.go publish PowerResume events, and let auto_update.go / api layer subscribe. This removes special-case callbacks and centralizes event dispatch. One platform-agnostic path: Subscribe(PowerResume, func() { RefreshAPIFunc(); scheduleAutoPing() }).

recommendations.title: Complete SPEC 047 phase 6: replace legacy callbacks with typed event subscriptions
recommendations.detail: core/controller.go:287-289 notes that UpdateConfigStatusFunc and UpdateCoreStatusFunc are 'temporary' placeholders until typed events mature. Currently ui/app.go and core_dashboard_tab.go wrap these callbacks for incremental migration. Phase 6 should: (1) publish ConfigBuilt and VpnStateChanged everywhere (already done for VpnStateChanged), (2) replace all callback assignments with EventBus.Subscribe, (3) remove the callback fields from UIService. This will unify the event model and simplify controller ↔ UI coupling.

recommendations.title: BUG1 (deriveV6FromLegacy) reflects dual-state / two sources of truth problem (SPEC 068)
recommendations.detail: deriveV6FromLegacy:438-449 is called on Load to backfill Rules/DNS from CustomRules/DNSOptions when the canonical fields are empty. This workaround masks a deeper architecture issue: legacy State.CustomRules + new State.Rules are maintained in parallel, creating two sources of truth. The root cause is that Load fills both views (legacy on v2-v5, derived on v6), but Save on headless paths (auto_update, log_level, config_service) doesn't update UI—it Load→mutate→Save without re-emitting from Configurator. Rule: Store ONLY Rules/DNS (canonical); derive legacy CustomRules/DNSOptions on-demand in UI layer (not in core/state). This eliminates BUG1 and forces callsites to converge. After Phase 7.

stateFlows: LOAD disk → memory: (1a) disk file → Parse detects schema (top-level version vs meta.version) → routes to v2/v3/v4/v5/v6 parser; (1b) v2-v4 → parseLegacyAndMigrate → migrateV4ToV5 → diskStateV5; (1c) v5 → parseV5Legacy reads diskStateV5; (1d) v6 → parseCurrent reads canonical Rules/DNS. (2) All paths: syncLegacyFromConnections fills ParserConfig.Proxies from Connections.Sources (backward-compat). (3) All paths: normalizeNilSlices, sanitizeOutboundRefs, deriveV6FromLegacy (BUG1 workaround on v2-v5). (4) Result: *State in memory with dual views (legacy ParserConfig + canonical Connections/Rules/DNS).

LOAD + PRESENTOR: (1) Configurator.LoadWizardState: state.Load → *State → refreshSourcesList (UI source_tab reads ParserConfig.Proxies). (2) Presenter.OpenWizardState: state.Rules → Rules tab, state.DNS → DNS tab, state.ConfigParams → Settings. (3) SyncDNSOptionsWithActivePresets called on load (presenter_state.go:127) to materialize kind=preset DNS entries from active Rule[kind=preset] refs.

EDIT + SAVE: (1) UI edits model.ParserConfig.ParserConfig.Proxies (source_tab, outbounds_tab) or model.Rules/model.DNS (Rules tab, DNS tab). (2) SaveCurrentState: state.Save(s) calls s.syncConnectionsFromLegacy (ParserConfig→Connections on Save), marshalDisk (v6 JSON layout), atomic write (tmp+Sync+Rename). (3) Result: disk state.json in v6 canonical form (Rules[], DNS flat with kind discriminator).

AUTO-UPDATE / HEADLESS: (1) core/auto_update.go: state.Load(statePath) → *State → refresh subscriptions → state.Save. (2) BUG1 manifests: deriveV6FromLegacy backfills Rules/DNS if empty (legacy v2-v5 path skips UI, so CustomRules→Rules not converted). (3) Next Load: legacyCustomRulesFromV6 converts Rules back to CustomRules for legacy view (circular dual-state).

MIGRATION v2→v5→v6: (1) parseLegacyAndMigrate: migrateV4ToV5 (IDGenerator optional), wrap in diskStateV5, then return State (ULID'ed Sources). (2) parseV5Legacy: diskStateV5 → State, call deriveV6FromLegacy to backfill Rules/DNS. (3) parseCurrent: diskStateV6 → State, legacyCustomRulesFromV6 derives CustomRules from Rules. (4) Edge case: legacyDevDNSToOptions reads old dev-shape `dns` field (SPEC 053), converts to canonical DNSOptions on load (one-shot, re-saves in new shape on next Save).

SNAPSHOT capture: (1) snapshot.Build(execDir, ...) → read 4 files via platform.GetWizardXPath. (2) Per file: os.ReadFile → jsonc.ToJSON (handles JSONC comments) → json.Valid → append to Snapshot.Files[] or Missing/Errors. (3) No errors raised; caller sees Files/Missing/Errors dict and decides severity.

dups.consolidation: Extract JSONBuilder struct with appendField(name, value) method to reduce boilerplate. Current approach error-prone for edge cases (nested objects, array handling). json.Marshal for leaf values already applied; wrapper can unify field ordering.

dups.desc: rule_set tag conversion logic duplicated: convertRuleSetToLocalRequired (route_merge.go:167) vs convertPresetRuleSetRemoteToLocal (preset_merge.go:42)
dups.consolidation: Merge into single convertRemoteRuleSetToLocal(rs, execDir, tag) that both callsites use. Local type=local and execDir logic identical; only differs in error message detail.

dups.desc: DNS-only fields stripping logic in stripDNSWizardOnlyFields duplicates pattern across MergeDNSSection and MergePresetsIntoDNS
dups.consolidation: Already consolidated in stripDNSWizardOnlyFields; no issue. Keep as single source of truth.

monoliths.problem: String-concat JSON generation across GenerateNodeJSON (248 LOC) and GenerateSelectorWithFilteredAddOutbounds (164 LOC) uses repeated fmt.Sprintf + strings.Join pattern. 1086 LOC total, with three-pass algorithm (buildOutboundsInfo, computeOutboundValidity, generateSelectorJSONs) and complex filter/expose tag logic interleaved. Difficult to test field ordering, handle new protocols (naive, etc. are cumbersome additions).
monoliths.decomposition: 1. Extract JSONBuilder{parts []string} helper with AppendField(name, value) — handles proper escaping via marshalJSONString. 2. Extract ProtocolFieldsHandler interface{GenerateFields(node) []string} — implement per protocol (VLESS, VMess, Trojan, etc.) to centralize field logic. 3. Move three-pass algorithm (buildOutboundsInfo/computeOutboundValidity/generateSelectorJSONs) to outbound_validity.go; GenerateOutboundsFromParserConfig becomes thin orchestrator. 4. Create filter_helpers.go for SelectorFiltersAcceptNode, filterNodesForSelector, matchesFilter logic (currently scattered).

monoliths.problem: Multi-step state coordination: UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions (186-306) handles cache refresh, per-source resilience, state mutation, UI callbacks, and auto-rebuild trigger. buildContextFromState (341-389) constructs BuildContext with DNS/Route/Preset merge contexts — deep nesting of optional parameters. RefreshSubscriptionsMetaAndCache (537-597), RefreshSourceInPlace (872-895), RefreshSingleSubscription (906-945) are three different per-source refresh paths with overlapping logic. Mixing concerns: subscription fetch orchestration, state persistence, UI signaling, build triggering.
monoliths.decomposition: 1. Extract SubscriptionFetcher service{FetchMeta(), RefreshAll(), RefreshOne()} — isolate per-source network I/O and meta management. 2. Extract ConfigContextBuilder{buildDNSConfig(), buildRouteConfig(), buildPresetContext()} — encapsulate BuildContext assembly logic separate from subscription handling. 3. Create StateCoordinator{UpdateState(), SaveState()} — single point for state.Load/mutate/Save + MarkConfigStale/ClearCacheStale. 4. Move UI callback chains into separate event service — decouple build triggers from refresh logic. Result: UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions becomes 50 LOC orchestrator calling these services in sequence.

recommendations.title: outbound_generator.go string-concat JSON: migrate to typed JSON builder
recommendations.detail: Current fmt.Sprintf + strings.Join is fragile for new protocol support (naive, hysteria2 recent additions show pattern). Extract JSONBuilder struct with AppendField(name, value) that marshals via json.Marshal internally + handles proper escaping. Test via golden outbound_generator_test.go. Risk: field ordering—ensure Builder preserves insertion order (use ordered map or slice of [key, value] pairs). Does NOT change behavior, only refactors internal implementation.

recommendations.title: Consolidate rule_set type conversion (local vs remote)
recommendations.detail: convertRuleSetToLocalRequired (route_merge.go:167) and convertPresetRuleSetRemoteToLocal (preset_merge.go:42) duplicate type=remote → type=local logic. Create single convertRuleSetToLocal(rs, execDir) that both paths use. Unify error messages. Tests in route_merge_test.go cover both paths after consolidation.

recommendations.title: Extract ResolveDNS/ResolveRoute shared metadata struct
recommendations.detail: ResolvedDNSServer + ResolvedDNSRule + ResolvedRouteRule all carry {Active, Enabled, InactiveReason}. Define shared ResolvedEntryMetadata struct, embed in each. Reduces code duplication in resolver implementations. No behavior change—purely structural.

recommendations.title: Extract config-build context assembly from config_service.go
recommendations.detail: buildContextFromState (lines 341-389) + dnsConfigForUpdate + routeConfigForUpdate are state-to-build-context transformations. Move to separate ConfigContextBuilder service. Improves testability: unit-test context assembly independently of subscription refresh orchestration. Risk: ensure BuildContext invariants preserved (e.g., Preset.ExecDir must be set for SRS resolution).

recommendations.title: Separate subscription fetching from state coordination in config_service.go
recommendations.detail: UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions conflates three concerns: (1) per-source network fetch + parse, (2) state.Load/mutate/Save, (3) UI signaling + auto-rebuild trigger. Extract SubscriptionFetcher service handling (1); leave config_service orchestrating (2)+(3). Risk: race conditions with SubscriptionMu must be preserved through new service boundaries. Test via existing update_*.go test suite. Payoff: clarifies retry/resilience logic, enables per-source fetch isolation (TODO in comment line 183).

recommendations.title: Create outbounds_validity.go: extract three-pass algorithm from outbound_generator.go
recommendations.detail: buildOutboundsInfo, computeOutboundValidity, generateSelectorJSONs (lines 692-908) are pure algorithm for determining which selectors are non-empty after dependency resolution. Move to separate file. GenerateOutboundsFromParserConfig becomes thin coordinator: load nodes → run validity pass → emit JSON. Improves readability of complex algorithm. Risk: ensure topological sort cycle-detection remains correct (existing WarnLog at line 835).

recommendations.title: Dead code audit: @ParserConfig block removal cleanup (SPEC 045)
recommendations.detail: Comments in build.go (lines 131-157) document removal of /** @ParserConfig */ block from config.json. Check for any legacy readers: grep -r '@ParserConfig' (should be zero in core, may have UI stubs). If found, remove dead parsing code. Current state: canonical source is state.json; config.json block was dub redundant since SPEC 045 cleanup.

recommendations.title: Document pipeline contracts: BuildContext → BuildConfig → Result
recommendations.detail: create docs/PIPELINE.md mapping state.json → BuildContext assembly → BuildConfig dispatch → per-section merge → config.json JSON. Helps future refactorers understand layering: (1) fetch/parse subscriptions → cache, (2) state.Load + cache assembly → BuildContext, (3) BuildConfig pure render, (4) atomicWrite. Current doc comments scattered; consolidate.

recommendations.title: Outbound JSON generation: separate protocol field logic from JSON formatting
recommendations.detail: GenerateNodeJSON (lines 242-489) mixes protocol-specific field extraction (UUID for vless, password for trojan, method for ss, etc.) with JSON formatting boilerplate. Create ProtocolFieldHandler interface; per-protocol impl in node_parser_vless.go style. Centralizes new-protocol additions (current: naive, hysteria2 recently added and are verbose). Risk: maintain field ordering invariants (tag, type, server, server_port, auth fields, tls, detour).

events.name: PresetActivation
subscribers: build/preset_expand.go ExpandPreset (via ExpandPreset call in resolve_*.go)

stateFlows: state.json → load → config_service.buildContextFromState → BuildContext.{Template,Vars,Cache,DNS,Route,Preset} → BuildConfig → sections per order → MergeDNSSection → MergePresetsIntoDNS (ResolveDNS) → emit
state.json → load → config_service.buildContextFromState → BuildContext.Route → MergeRouteSection → MergePresetsIntoRoute (ResolveRoute + ExpandPreset per preset-ref) → emit rules + rule_sets

subscription fetch → GenerateOutboundsFromParserConfig (three passes: buildOutboundsInfo, computeOutboundValidity, generateSelectorJSONs) → []string JSON → cache → BuildContext.Cache → BuildOutboundsSection (add @ParserSTART markers) → emit

packages.responsibility: Protocol parsers + share-URI encoders for proxy nodes (VLESS, VMess, Trojan, SS, Hysteria2, SSH, SOCKS, WireGuard, Naive) and subscription transport (fetch + decode + metadata extraction)

keyFiles.purpose: Entry point: fetches & parses subscriptions (URL → nodes), applies tag filters/naming, respects cache hook for offline rebuild
Main parser: dispatches URI scheme → protocol parser, extracts metadata (tag/label/comment), builds sing-box outbound JSON
Reverse of ParseNode: outbound JSON → shareable proxy URI (VLESS/Trojan/SS/VMess/Hysteria2/SSH/Naive/WireGuard)
HTTP fetch + announce-header decode: downloads subscriptions with configurable User-Agent & HWID headers, surfaces provider messages
Metadata parsing: extracts headers (Subscription-Userinfo, Profile-Title, Profile-Update-Interval) and inline #comments; handles provider announce on empty body (HWID-limit, region-block)
Content decoding: base64 → plain text URIs or JSON array Xray configs; handles multiple encoding variants
Transport builder: queryGetFold + uriTransportFromQuery → sing-box V2Ray transport (WS/gRPC/HTTP/HTTPUpgrade); TLS extraction (REALITY/plain)
Xray JSON array parser: converts Xray outbound format (vnext/settings) + jump-proxies into ParsedNode + sing-box Outbound

packages.responsibility: Clash API client: proxies list + delay test + group switch (remote server management via Clash API)

keyFiles.purpose: Clash API HTTP layer: LoadClashAPIConfig + GetProxiesInGroup + SwitchProxy + GetDelay; handles net errors (timeout → ErrPlatformInterrupt), logging to api.log

packages.responsibility: Debug HTTP API: introspect + control app state (run/stop, fetch proxies, probe delays, rebuild config, traffic profiling)

keyFiles.purpose: HTTP server scaffold: auth (Bearer token), routes (/version, /state/*, /traffic/*, /action/*), facade to AppController
Traffic Profiler endpoints: /traffic/status + /live + /sessions (SPEC 059), wraps internal/traffic.TrafficProfiler singleton

recommendations.title: Break apart node_parser.go (744 lines) into scheme-specific modules
recommendations.detail: ParseNode function is complex and dispatches ~10 schemes (VLESS/VMess/Trojan/SS/Hysteria2/SSH/SOCKS/WireGuard/Naive/Shadowsocks). Decompose into: (1) node_parser_core.go (scheme detection + common logic: URI validation, label/tag extraction), (2) node_parser_vmess.go (already exists, but move VMess specifics here), (3) node_parser_ss.go (Shadowsocks base64 decode + SIP002), (4) node_parser_socks.go (SOCKS5/SOCKS parsing), (5) move WireGuard/Naive/Hysteria2 logic to existing files more cleanly. ParseNode becomes a thin router: detect scheme → call scheme-module → buildOutbound. This drops cyclomatic complexity and makes per-protocol testing clearer. Target: each file <300 lines.

recommendations.title: Consolidate transport + TLS parsing helpers into node_parser_transport.go module
recommendations.detail: Current state: queryGetFold, normalizePercentDecodeLoop, normalizeRealityShortID, vlessTLSFromNode, trojanTLSFromNode are in node_parser_transport.go; uriTransportFromQuery is also here. But node_parser.go imports node_parser_transport.go + calls vlessTLSFromNode etc. Rename node_parser_transport.go → node_parser_query_helpers.go (or keep name) and clearly document it as the central module for query-string → sing-box-config-object conversion. Add a TransportAndTLS struct/module (buildTransportAndTLS) that centralizes the logic for TLS + transport together (they are often co-dependent: TLS security=none vs TLS+UTLS). This will make it easier to add new protocols.

recommendations.title: Extract share_uri_encode.go protocol-specific logic into shareuri_*.go files
recommendations.detail: share_uri_encode.go (883 lines) has functions shareURIFromVLESS, shareURIFromVMess, ..., shareURIFromNaive. Split into: shareuri_vless.go, shareuri_vmess.go, shareuri_trojan.go, shareuri_ss.go, shareuri_hysteria2.go, shareuri_ssh.go, shareuri_socks.go, shareuri_wireguard.go, shareuri_naive.go. Keep common helpers (mapGetString, mapGetInt, transportToQuery, vlessTLSToQuery, etc.) in shareuri_helpers.go. ShareURIFromOutbound becomes a thin dispatch in shareuri.go. This mirrors the pattern already used in node_parser_*.go files. Reduces cognitive load per file to ~100 lines.

recommendations.title: Deduplicate transport parsing across uriTransportFromQuery and xrayTransportFromStreamSettings
recommendations.detail: Two separate parsers exist: (1) uriTransportFromQuery in node_parser_transport.go (handles subscription URI query params: type=ws/grpc/http/httpupgrade), (2) xrayTransportFromStreamSettings in xray_outbound_convert.go (handles Xray JSON: wsSettings/grpcSettings/httpSettings). Both build the same sing-box {type, path, service_name, host} structure but from different input formats. Create a shared module transport_builder.go with: (1) generic TransportSpec type (union of all fields), (2) buildFromQueryParams (current uriTransportFromQuery logic), (3) buildFromXray (current xrayTransportFromStreamSettings logic), (4) toSingboxJSON (output formatting). This prevents drift when sing-box transport schema changes.

recommendations.title: Unify TLS parsing (vlessTLSFromNode vs xrayVLESSTLSFromStreamSettings)
recommendations.detail: Two VLESS TLS parsers: (1) vlessTLSFromNode (node_parser_transport.go) reads from URL query (security, pbk, sid, fp), (2) xrayVLESSTLSFromStreamSettings (xray_outbound_convert.go) reads from Xray JSON (tlsSettings, realitySettings). Same outputs but different input translation. Create tls_builder.go: (1) struct TLSSpec (enabled, server_name, utls, reality, insecure, alpn), (2) buildFromURIQuery, (3) buildFromXrayJSON, (4) toSingboxJSON. Use shared normalizeRealityShortID, NormalizeUTLSFingerprint, applyTLSQueryExtras. This avoids bugs where one parser gets updated (e.g., new REALITY field) and the other doesn't.

recommendations.title: Add error context wrapper for net.Error mapping in clash.go
recommendations.detail: classifyRequestError function (clash.go:266) maps context.Canceled/net.Error/OpError → user messages. But the same logic is repeated across GetProxiesInGroup, SwitchProxy, GetDelay (3 copies). Extract: type RequestError struct {Err error, URL string} → wrap in GET/PUT call sites → classifyRequestError receives RequestError and has context for logging. This avoids the fallbackFormat parameter pattern and makes error reporting more consistent (currently 'switch request' vs 'proxies request' vs 'delay request' vary in text).

recommendations.title: Migrate fetcher.go function-pointer hooks to dependency-injection interface
recommendations.detail: Current pattern: CreateHTTPClientFunc, IsNetworkErrorFunc, GetNetworkErrorMessageFunc, LoadSubscriptionSettingsFunc are package-level func pointers set by core init (config_service.go). This makes testing hard (requires global state mutations) and implicit dependencies. Extract an interface SubscriptionDependencies { CreateHTTPClient(), IsNetworkError(), GetNetworkErrorMessage(), LoadSettings() } and pass it to FetchSubscriptionWithMeta. Create a testfakes package with mock implementations. This will also help if fetcher ever needs to be reused in headless/CLI contexts.

recommendations.title: Consolidate node-skipping logic (shouldSkipNode pattern)
recommendations.detail: shouldSkipNode (node_parser.go:529) reads skipFilters []map[string]string and evaluates regex. Same pattern is likely in core/config/configtypes.MatchesPattern (imported in node_parser.go:525). Extract a SkipFilter type with methods: Matches(node *ParsedNode) bool. This allows: (1) unit tests on SkipFilter alone, (2) future expansions (whitelist filters, tag-based filters), (3) clearer intent in LoadNodesFromSource where skip logic is currently mixed with tag/dedup logic.

recommendations.title: Separate debugapi state-mutation concerns (LoadState/SaveState vs API handlers)
recommendations.detail: debugapi/server.go imports core/state for SPEC 053+ endpoints. But ControllerFacade.LoadState/SaveState are generic state I/O hooks used by multiple endpoints. Extract a StateManager interface (LoadState, SaveState, ApplyLogLevel) that the facade implements, so debugapi can be tested without the full controller. This also clarifies that /state/* endpoints are read-only query or atomic mutations, not ad-hoc edits.

recommendations.title: Mark subscription cache hook (LookupCachedBody) as deprecated; migrate to explicit parameter
recommendations.detail: source_loader.go:14-27 documents LookupCachedBody as a SPEC 052 feature: caller (Update/Rebuild in core) sets a hook so fetcher doesn't re-fetch. But this is a package-level global, making concurrency/testing fragile. Plan: Add optional CachedBodyFunc to FetchSubscriptionWithMeta signature (breaking change on next refactor cycle). For now, add a deprecation warning in the godoc and note in SPEC 070 cleanup checklist.

recommendations.title: Clean up UTF-8 validation duplicates (validateAndFixUTF8 appears twice)
recommendations.detail: node_parser.go:420-429 define validateAndFixUTF8 (string → string) and validateAndFixUTF8Bytes ([]byte → string). Similar logic exists in decoder.go, meta.go. Extract a utf8_utils.go with: FixUTF8(s string) string, FixUTF8Bytes(b []byte) string, ValidateUTF8Strict(s string, allowedControlRunes ...) error. This makes the intent clearer (especially the 'strict' variant that rejects control-chars for display).

recommendations.title: Document transport query param case-insensitivity invariant in a shared type
recommendations.detail: queryGetFold is used 40+ times across node_parser*.go. The naming (Fold) is from RFC 7230 but not obvious to new readers. Add a package-level comment in node_parser_query_helpers.go documenting: 'Query strings in proxy URIs are case-insensitive per [ref]; use queryGetFold for all reads.' Add a TODO for Go 1.25+: url.Values.GetFold(name string) might be added upstream, at which point we can drop our helper and use stdlib.

dups.desc: UTF-8 validation and repair: validateAndFixUTF8 (node_parser.go:420), validateAndFixUTF8Bytes (node_parser.go:433), hasControlChars (meta.go:406), sanitizeForDisplay (node_parser.go:452)
dups.consolidation: Extract utf8_utils.go module: FixUTF8String(s string) (string, bool), FixUTF8Bytes(b []byte) (string, bool), HasControlChars(s string) bool. All callers import from this module. Reduces duplication and makes policy consistent (currently meta.go and node_parser.go have slightly different control-char definitions).

dups.desc: Base64 decode with padding fallback: decodeBase64WithPadding (node_parser.go:378), tryDecodeBase64 (decoder.go:15), tryBase64Decode (meta.go:381)
dups.consolidation: Create encoding_utils.go: DecodeBase64Multi(s string) ([]byte, string, error) returns (decoded, source, err). Source indicates which variant succeeded (useful for logging). Replace all 3 functions with calls to this one. Also consolidates the 4-variant try loop (URL-safe padded/unpadded, Std padded/unpadded) in one place.

dups.desc: Transport builder from query params: uriTransportFromQuery (node_parser_transport.go:65) vs xrayTransportFromStreamSettings (xray_outbound_convert.go:214)
dups.consolidation: Create transport_builder.go: type TransportSpec (union of {type, path, service_name, host, headers}). Implement (1) buildFromQueryParams (uriTransportFromQuery logic), (2) buildFromXrayJSON (xrayTransportFromStreamSettings logic), (3) toSingboxMap() → map[string]interface{}. Both input formats → same spec → same output. Prevents drift when sing-box transport schema evolves.

dups.desc: TLS config builder: vlessTLSFromNode (node_parser_transport.go:182) vs xrayVLESSTLSFromStreamSettings (xray_outbound_convert.go:144) vs trojanTLSFromNode (node_parser_transport.go:254)
dups.consolidation: Create tls_builder.go: type TLSSpec (enabled, server_name, utls, reality, insecure, alpn). Implement (1) buildFromVLESSQuery (VLESS URI), (2) buildFromTrojanQuery (Trojan URI), (3) buildFromXrayJSON (Xray JSON). Centralize normalizeRealityShortID, applyTLSQueryExtras, NormalizeUTLSFingerprint. Ensures both subscription parsers and Xray converters stay in sync when TLS fields are added (e.g., new ALPN format).

dups.desc: Net error classification: classifyRequestError (clash.go:266) logic repeated in GetProxiesInGroup/SwitchProxy/GetDelay error handling
dups.consolidation: Extract RequestErrorMapper type: (err error, context string) → UserErrorMessage. All three functions (GetProxiesInGroup, SwitchProxy, GetDelay) call mapper.Classify(err, 'request type') instead of inline classifyRequestError. This centralizes error message formatting and makes it testable. Also reduces timestamp-logging duplication (currently each function has writeLog calls with similar timestamp patterns).

dups.desc: Query parameter case-insensitive lookup: queryGetFold (node_parser_transport.go:12) duplicated pattern in node_parser_hysteria2.go (queryGetFold call) and node_parser_vmess.go
dups.consolidation: All three files import node_parser_transport and call queryGetFold (already centralized). No action needed. Document in node_parser_transport.go that this is the canonical helper for all subscription URI query parsing (cross-package standardization point).

monoliths.problem: Single ParseNode function (lines 40-370) dispatches 10 proxy protocols (VLESS, VMess, Trojan, SS, Hysteria2, SSH, SOCKS5, WireGuard, Naive, Shadowsocks). Complex scheme detection + parsing + UTF-8 validation + skip-filter logic interwoven. 25+ helper functions below ParseNode deal with specific schemes or generic tasks (UTF-8 fix, tag extraction, outbound building). High cyclomatic complexity per protocol; testing each scheme in isolation is awkward.
monoliths.decomposition: Split into: (1) node_parser_core.go (ParseNode dispatcher, IsDirectLink, MaxURILength, common helpers: extractTagAndComment, normalizeFlagTag, generateDefaultTag, buildOutbound as a generic; 200 lines), (2) node_parser_ss.go (Shadowsocks base64 decode + SIP002 + legacy format; 150 lines), (3) node_parser_socks.go (SOCKS/SOCKS5 parsing; 100 lines), (4) consolidate node_parser_vmess.go + node_parser_naive.go + node_parser_hysteria2.go to be cleaner per-protocol. Move UTF-8 utils to utf8_utils.go (shared), move tag/comment extraction to tag_utils.go (shared). Result: node_parser_core.go is a thin router, each protocol module handles ParseNode branch → configtypes.ParsedNode. Each file becomes testable in isolation. Cyclomatic complexity drops from ~20 to ~5 per module.

monoliths.problem: Monolithic reverse-encoder: 20+ protocol-specific functions (shareURIFromVLESS, shareURIFromVMess, shareURIFromTrojan, shareURIFromShadowsocks, shareURIFromSocks, shareURIFromHysteria2, shareURIFromSSH, shareURIFromNaive, ShareURIFromWireGuardEndpoint) plus 10+ helpers (mapGetString, mapGetInt, mapGetBool, transportToQuery, vlessTLSToQuery, shareAppendALPNInsecure, trojanTLSToQuery, wireGuardPeerMaps, stringSliceFromWireGuardField, wireGuardJSONElemToString, wireGuardDNSToQuery). Hard to navigate; each protocol's round-trip contract (outbound → URI) is scattered.
monoliths.decomposition: Reorganize into: (1) share_uri.go (ShareURIFromOutbound dispatcher; 50 lines), (2) shareuri_vless.go (shareURIFromVLESS, vlessTLSToQuery, transport parsing; 100 lines), (3) shareuri_vmess.go (shareURIFromVMess; 100 lines), (4) shareuri_trojan.go (shareURIFromTrojan, trojanTLSToQuery; 80 lines), (5) shareuri_ss.go (shareURIFromShadowsocks; 40 lines), (6) shareuri_socks.go (shareURIFromSocks; 40 lines), (7) shareuri_hysteria2.go (shareURIFromHysteria2, hysteria2ServerPortsToMportQuery; 80 lines), (8) shareuri_ssh.go (shareURIFromSSH; 80 lines), (9) shareuri_naive.go (shareURIFromNaive; 80 lines), (10) shareuri_wireguard.go (ShareURIFromWireGuardEndpoint + helpers; 120 lines), (11) shareuri_helpers.go (mapGetString, mapGetInt, mapGetBool, fragmentFromTag, hostPort, shareAppendDetourLiteral, transportToQuery, shareAppendALPNInsecure; 150 lines). Each file now ~100 lines; protocol authors can find round-trip logic in one place. Easier to add new protocols.

monoliths.problem: LoadNodesFromSource (lines 83-312) is the entry point for subscription ingestion. 230 lines of logic: HTTP fetch (delegated to FetchSubscription), Xray JSON vs plaintext detection, skip-filter application, tag prefix/postfix/mask substitution, dedup logic, progress callback. Mixed concerns: parsing orchestration, progress reporting, tag manipulation, dedup, caching. Hard to test individual steps; progress callback adds noise.
monoliths.decomposition: Extract: (1) LoadNodesFromSource stays but shrinks to 100 lines: fetch → detect format → parse lines/JSON → apply-tags-and-dedup; (2) create SourceSubscriptionParser interface { ParseLineFormat(body []byte) ([]*ParsedNode, error), ParseJSONFormat(body []byte) ([]*ParsedNode, error) }, inject implementations → LoadNodesFromSource calls parser methods. (3) Extract TagNormalizer struct { ApplyPrefix(prefix, node, num), ApplyPostfix(postfix, node, num), ApplyMask(mask, node, num), MakeUnique(tag, counts) } used in a separate step. (4) Extract ProgressReporter { Report(pct float64, msg string) } and default no-op impl; LoadNodesFromSource doesn't log directly. (5) Move applyTagsToXrayNode → XrayNodeTagger struct in xray_outbound_convert.go. Result: LoadNodesFromSource becomes a 5-step pipeline that's testable end-to-end or per-step.

monoliths.problem: Single monolithic file for Clash API: HTTP client lifecycle (clashHTTPClient, ResetClashHTTPTransport, getHTTPClient with lock), logging (SetAPILogFile, SetAPILogSink, writeLog), error classification (classifyRequestError, normalizeRequestError), three request handlers (GetProxiesInGroup ~130 lines, SwitchProxy ~50 lines, GetDelay ~60 lines), plus config loading + ping endpoint management. Mixed layers: transport, auth, error mapping, application logic (proxy list, delay test) all in one file.
monoliths.decomposition: Split into: (1) clash_config.go (LoadClashAPIConfig; 50 lines), (2) clash_transport.go (clashHTTPClient, ResetClashHTTPTransport, getHTTPClient, httpIdleConnTimeout logic; 50 lines), (3) clash_log.go (SetAPILogFile, SetAPILogSink, ClearAPILogSink, writeLog, apiLogFile/apiLogSink guards; 50 lines), (4) clash_error.go (classifyRequestError, normalizeRequestError, RequestErrorClassifier type; 50 lines), (5) clash_proxy.go (GetProxiesInGroup, ProxyInfo type, DisplayOrName, ContextMenuTypeLine; 150 lines), (6) clash_switch.go (SwitchProxy; 50 lines), (7) clash_delay.go (GetDelay, TestAPIConnection, SetPingTestURL/AllConcurrency; 100 lines). Each file has one concern; shared logMsg utility for timestamp consistency. Package init wires defaults. Result: when a protocol provider changes (e.g., new proxy type field in /proxies response), the change localizes to clash_proxy.go.

stateFlows: User → ConfigWizard adds subscription URL → LoadNodesFromSource invokes FetchSubscription(url) → fetcher.go:FetchSubscriptionWithMeta sends HTTP GET (with HWID/UA headers) → reads response body (max 10MB) → parses headers (Profile-Title, Announce, etc.) + inline #comments → merges meta → DecodeSubscriptionContent (base64 or plain) → returns decoded body + FetchResult {Body, RawBody, Meta, HTTPStatus}

Decoded subscription body (plain text URIs per line) → LoadNodesFromSource splits lines → ParseNode(line) for each → dispatches by scheme prefix (vless://, vmess://, ss://, etc.) → protocol-specific parser extracts host/port/UUID/query-params → buildOutbound constructs sing-box JSON → applies tag filters + prefix/postfix/mask + dedup → returns []*ParsedNode

Xray JSON array subscription (alternative format) → LoadNodesFromSource detects [{ protocol, settings, streamSettings, ... }] → ParseNodesFromXrayJSONArray → loops outbounds → xrayBuildVLESSFromOutbound (vnext+users) + xrayTransportFromStreamSettings (WS/gRPC/HTTP) + xrayVLESSTLSFromStreamSettings (TLS/Reality) → ParsedNode.Outbound → tags applied same as line-based

Jump proxy (Xray chain): outbound has dialerProxy reference → xrayBuildJumpFromOutbound reads referenced socks/vless outbound → parses server/port/auth → returns ParsedJump (intermediate proxy) → embedded in parent ParsedNode.Jump for chained routing

Tag naming: node parsed from subscription, tag extracted from label (fragment or comment) or generated (vless-host-port) → prefix/postfix/mask applied (variable substitution: {$tag}, {$protocol}, {$num}) → dedup: if tag exists in map, append -2/-3/... → normalized for display (emoji fixes) → stored in ParsedNode.Tag + Outbound['tag']

Share URI (reverse): ParsedNode stored in state → UI export button → ShareURIFromOutbound reads outbound map (tag, type, server, port, uuid, transport, tls, password, etc.) → dispatches by type → shareURIFromVLESS builds url.URL (scheme=vless, user=uuid, host=server:port, query=security/sni/fp/flow/etc, fragment=tag) → URL.String() → shared via clipboard/QR

Clash API workflow: Servers tab → GetProxiesInGroup(baseURL, token, groupName) → GET /proxies → parses {proxies: {groupName: {all: [proxyNames], now: activeName, ...}}} → returns []ProxyInfo (Name, DisplayName, ClashType, Traffic, Delay from history) → UI renders list; user clicks proxy → SwitchProxy(baseURL, token, groupName, proxyName) → PUT /proxies/{groupName} {name: proxyName} → Clash updates active

Delay test: user clicks 'Test' on a proxy → GetDelay(baseURL, token, proxyName) → GET /proxies/{proxyName}/delay?url=PingTestEndpoint.URL&timeout=5000 → Clash measures latency → returns {delay: ms} → stored in ProxyInfo.Delay

Debug API: external tool (CI, script, agent) → GET /ping (127.0.0.1:9263) → OK, or GET /version with Bearer token → returns launcher version + sing-box version, or POST /action/update-subs → background fetch all subscriptions → returns 202, or GET /state/full → LoadState → serialize state.json → returns {rules: [...], dns: {...}, outbounds: [...]} for programmatic config inspection

Auto-rebuild on cache-refresh success (line 286): Update refreshes subscription cache, then auto-rebuilds config.json so it stays in sync. Best-effort: rebuild error does not cancel Update success.

monoliths.problem: Monolithic config update: UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions (186 lines) handles subscription meta refresh, network fetch, generator invocation, state save, dirty-marker application, auto-rebuild call, and UI callback. monoliths.decomposition: Extract internal helpers: (1) refreshSubscriptionsMetaAndCacheInternal() handles meta+raw fetch only; (2) applyGenerationResultAndDirtyMarkers() handles generator result → state save → dirty flags; (3) UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions becomes orchestrator calling (1) then (2) then auto-rebuild. Consider moving generator invocation to ConfigService method that wraps config.GenerateOutboundsFromParserConfig (currently duplicated in GenerateOutboundsFromParserConfig + GenerateNodeJSON).

monoliths.problem: AppController monolith: 747 lines, 113 fields (services, process state, run state, caches, mutexes, state machine flags, timers, event handlers, auto-update retry timers). Responsibilities bleed: process lifecycle, config parsing, API state, UI callbacks, version caching, event bridging, auto-update loop startup, DI wiring. Dense state: CmdMutex locks 11+ fields; ParserMutex, RunningState.RWMutex, SubscriptionMu, CmdMutex are independent locks (deadlock risk).
monoliths.decomposition: Decompose into: (1) ProcessLifecycleManager (SingboxCmd, SingboxPrivilegedMode/PID, StoppedByUser, RestartRequestedByUser, ConsecutiveCrashAttempts, RunningState + crash/restart state machine); (2) CacheManager (installedCoreVersionCache, autoUpdateRetryTimers, autoUpdateEventLastFetch, SubscriptionMu + dirty-marker application); (3) Keep AppController as thin orchestrator holding services + EventBus + UI callbacks. Lock strategy: ProcessLifecycleManager has CmdMutex, CacheManager has SubscriptionMu + version lock, no cross-locking.

monoliths.problem: ProcessService.Monitor(cmd) (107 lines, 3 goto labels, 5 nested if/check branches, GOLDEN STANDARD comment indicates fragility): monolithic crash/restart handler. monoliths.decomposition: Extract CrashHandler struct (owns crash counter, restart flag, stability threshold, max attempts); implement unified Decide(isPrivileged, exitErr, flags) → Action{Kind: StoppedByUser | RestartRequested | CrashRestart | Max | OK}. Call from Monitor(cmd) and onPrivilegedScriptExited(scriptPID) with the same logic. Move all UI dialogs + timeouts to dedicated PostCrashAction(action, ac) function. Separate concern: process tree watching (isSingBoxProcessRunning + PID tracking) into ProcessWatcher type.

recommendations.title: Refactor ProcessService crash/restart state machine: extract unified CrashHandler
recommendations.detail:
Monitor() (lines 371-478) and onPrivilegedScriptExited() (lines 303-369) duplicate 50+ lines of crash/restart logic (attempt counter, restart request flag, stability timer, dialog, delay). Extract CrashHandler struct with Decide(isPrivileged, exitErr, StoppedByUser, RestartRequested, counter) → Action enum; implement PostCrashAction(action) separately. Both code paths call the same unified handler.

This unblocks: (1) easier testing of crash behavior; (2) fixes potential flag-check order bugs (lines 384-425 vs 305-335 might diverge); (3) enables future fixes (e.g. exponential backoff, jitter) in one place.

recommendations.title: Delete GetController fallback (lines 140-165), enforce NewAppController at startup
recommendations.detail:
GetController() creates a half-initialized controller if instance=nil, with bare services lacking callbacks/wiring (ProcessService, ConfigService, StateService but no UIService, no EventBus proper init). This drift (vs NewAppController lines 197-303) violates single initialization point.

Action: (1) Delete fallback code (lines 142-165). (2) Ensure main.go calls NewAppController before any GetController (already does, line 48). (3) Replace any package-level GetController() calls with dependency injection (e.g. in tests). (4) Optionally add GetControllerOrPanic() that fails fast if nil.

recommendations.title: Consolidate DI callback initialization (RefreshAPIFunc, UpdateCoreStatusFunc, etc)
recommendations.detail:
No-op handlers defined in both controller.go:274-281 AND uiservice/ui_service.go:126-140 with identical 'debuglog.DebugLog("handler is not set yet")' placeholders. Current pattern: handlers initialized to no-op logging, then UI sets real ones. Problem: log messages confuse debugging ('handler not set' still logs even when IS set).

Action: (1) Single source of truth: define DefaultUICallbacks in uiservice package, import in controller. OR (2) Accept handlers may be nil, wrap all call sites with nil-check (if ac.UIService.RefreshAPIFunc != nil). Recommendation: (2) — simpler, avoids double-initialization.

recommendations.title: Formalize Start/Build/Save state machine with explicit documentation + test
recommendations.detail:
Config.json invariant (SPEC 045): config.json is ONLY written by RebuildConfigIfDirty. But the wiring is implicit across three functions: Start() calls RebuildConfigIfDirty (line 154), UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions() calls RebuildConfigIfDirty (line 286), and RebuildConfigIfDirty has noop fast-path (line 154).

Action: (1) Add top-level docstring in controller.go explaining: 'config.json lifecycle = RebuildConfigIfDirty is sole writer. Start() rebuilds before exec if dirty (pre-start hook). Update() auto-rebuilds on cache success. RebuildConfigIfDirty noop-skips if clean & not forced.' (2) Add integration test: (a) dirty markers clean → Start() calls rebuild → noop; (b) Update() → CacheStale true, ConfigStale true; (c) RebuildConfigIfDirty forced=false → noop (already rebuilt); (d) Start() rebuilds; (e) verify config.json written once per Rebuild.

recommendations.title: Decompose AppController monolith (747 lines, 113 fields, 4 independent mutexes)
recommendations.detail:
AppController mixes process lifecycle (SingboxCmd, CmdMutex, StoppedByUser, RestartRequestedByUser, ConsecutiveCrashAttempts, RunningState), cache management (autoUpdateRetryTimers, autoUpdateEventLastFetch, installedCoreVersionCache + locks), and DI wiring (services, EventBus, callbacks). Deep lock interactions: SubscriptionMu serializes meta-refresh + Rebuild; CmdMutex locks process + state flags; ParserMutex gates parser. Risk: deadlock under concurrent Update + Start, race windows in RunningState.Set.

Action: Extract ProcessLifecycleManager (owns process state + Monitor/Stop/Start orchestration + CmdMutex), CacheManager (owns subscription timers + version cache + SubscriptionMu). AppController keeps services + EventBus + UI callbacks (thin orchestrator).

recommendations.title: Simplify ConfigService monolith: extract subscription meta/cache refresh, generator result application
recommendations.detail:
UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions (186 lines, ~5 indents): mixes subscription meta refresh (lines 190-203), generator setup (lines 205-246), error handling (lines 247-259), dirty-marker application (lines 264-280), auto-rebuild (lines 282-289), toast emission (lines 291-301). Deep nesting: subscription.LookupCachedBody hook + prevHook restore inside generator loop.

Action: Extract (1) refreshSubscriptionsMetaAndCacheInternal() — does meta refresh + raw cache only; (2) applyGenerationResultAndMarkers(result, rebuildErr) — applies dirty flags + auto-rebuild + toast. UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions becomes thin orchestrator calling these two.

recommendations.title: Add explicit documentation linking Start/pre-rebuild-hook with SPEC 068 (Wizard Save) invariant
recommendations.detail:
Wizard Save (SPEC 068) raises CacheStale/ConfigStale dirty markers. Start() calls RebuildConfigIfDirty (line 154) to apply them BEFORE sing-box runs. This contract is implicit and fragile: if any caller skips RebuildConfigIfDirty before Start, running process sees stale config.

Action: Add docstring to Start() explaining: 'Pre-start rebuild hook (SPEC 068): Wizard Save sets dirty markers; before launching sing-box, we rebuild config.json so running process has fresh template changes (TUN, DNS, rules, etc).' Cross-reference in Wizard Save handler and RebuildConfigIfDirty.

recommendations.title: Unify process-running check (isSingBoxProcessRunning duplication across Monitor + Stop + CheckIfRunningAtStart)
recommendations.detail:
ProcessService.isSingBoxProcessRunning (lines 693-759) called from Monitor (via race-check PID compare), Stop (implicit via ac.SingboxCmd), CheckIfRunningAtStart (line 616). Two variants: Windows (tasklist CSV) vs PS library fallback. Minor duplication: parseCSVLine + process.GetProcesses both called from multiple paths, logic slightly different (ourPID check, heuristic matching).

Action: (1) Consolidate into single ProcessWatcher type with cached process list. (2) Separate concerns: FindSingBoxProcess(ourPID) returns (found, pid); IsSingBoxProcessRunning() calls it. (3) Cache for ~100ms to avoid repeated system calls within same event loop tick.

stateFlows: main.go NewAppController → (FileService → UIService → APIService → ProcessService → ConfigService → StateService + EventBus) → startAutoUpdateLoop (1h heartbeat + event subscriptions)

user clicks Connect → StartSingBoxProcess → ProcessService.Start → RebuildConfigIfDirty (pre-check hook) → Launch sing-box + Monitor goroutine → RunningState.Set(true) → UpdateUI + autoPingTimer arm → AutoLoadProxies scheduled

Sing-box crashes → Monitor detects (cmd.Wait err) → Check PID/StoppedByUser/RestartRequested → ConsecutiveCrashAttempts++ → Delay 2s + ghostTunCleanup(true) → Start(true) → stability timer (180s) → if stable reset counter
Wizard Save + dirty markers → (CacheStale/ConfigStale raised via StateService) → Next Start() triggers RebuildConfigIfDirty → parses + builds config.json (SPEC 045 invariant: sole writer)

Manual Update button → ConfigService.UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions → subscription meta refresh + network fetch → cache per-source .raw → ClearCacheStale + MarkConfigStale → auto-RebuildConfigIfDirty → toast with ShowSubsResultFunc

Auto-update heartbeat (1h) → Auto-update loop wakes → runScheduledRefresh per-source staleness check → stale sources → refreshSourceWithRetry (with event cooldown 5s, retry 15s) → on failure retry timer scheduled
Power resume (macOS/Windows) → ResetClashHTTPTransport → schedule UI refresh + auto-ping-after-connect (soft-cap check) 2s later (if enabled + under max-proxies)
AppExit → GracefulExit → cancel ac.ctx (signal autoUpdateLoop, autoPingTimer, retryTimers) → StopSingBoxProcess → wait 2s for RunningState.IsRunning() clear → close log files

Outbound resolution: GetAvailableOutbounds (memoized), ResolveMergedOutbound; preset outbound tag collection; ensures default tags (direct-out, reject, drop)
Local proxy group markers (WIZARD:auto, WIZARD:selector): tag generation, conflict detection; state lifecycle for ProxySource outbounds
Input validation: ParserConfig, URL, URI, outbounds, JSON size; used on Save, parsing entry points

Benefits: per-domain testing, easier code review, clearer mental model of DNS state lifecycle.

recommendations.title: Enforce strict presentation ↔ business boundary: no cross-layer types
recommendations.detail: Presentation imports business (UIUpdater), business imports models (WizardModel) — correct. Verify no business → presentation imports exist (audit showed none). Consider: (a) formalizing UIUpdater as the ONLY bridge, (b) moving UIUpdater to models package so business doesn't import presentation package at all (currently fine, but explicit is clearer), (c) documenting in ARCHITECTURE.md that business layer is unit-testable without Fyne. Currently sound, but formalize as lint rule.

recommendations.title: Add test coverage for UI dispatch contract: SafeFyneDo + UpdateUI coordination
recommendations.detail: Current contract (presenter_sync.go:23-25): fyne.Do only through SafeFyneDo/UpdateUI; WizardWidgetsReady guards early (reader-side); programmatic flags (SourceURLsProgrammatic, DNSSelectsProgrammatic, etc.) suppress MarkAsChanged. Add unit tests: (1) UpdateUI → fyne.Do barrier test, (2) WizardWidgetsReady state machine, (3) programmatic flag suppression. Validates dispatch model against race conditions.

recommendations.title: Document SPEC 068 (presentation = orchestration) and SPEC 070 (clean layering) decisions in ARCHITECTURE.md
recommendations.detail: Add section: MVP pattern (model ↔ presenter ↔ view), UIUpdater interface as bridge, UI dispatch via SafeFyneDo, three concerns (presentation/business/models) and their separation. Include: (a) why presenter orchestrates (single source of truth for model mutations), (b) why business never imports Fyne, (c) UIUpdater as inversion-of-control bridge. Clarifies refactoring scope for future work.

Propose: internal/ui/callback_coordinator.go with RegisterCoreCallbacks(tab *CoreDashboardTab, uiService *core.UIService), RegisterClashAPICallbacks(controller *core.AppController) — centralizes lifecycle logic and makes callback contracts explicit.

recommendations.title: Break up CreateCoreDashboardTab into functional sub-builders
recommendations.detail: core_dashboard_tab.go:94-207 spans 113 LOC with 5 nested block creations (statusRow, versionBlock, wintunBlock, configBlock, stateBlock) and 5 callback registrations. While not technically a monolith (callbacks are registered, not inline), the function is a "God Coordinator" mixing layout logic with lifecycle setup. Propose: extract createStatusRow (lines 210-337 → separate), createVersionBlock (lines 441-506 → separate), createWintunBlock (already separate 1309-1373), createConfigBlock (already separate 339-438), createStateBlock (already separate 746-768) so CreateCoreDashboardTab becomes: row := createStatusRow(tab); version := createVersionBlock(tab); ... then compose container + registerCallbacks(). This shifts the function from 113 LOC of nested closures to clear sequential logic.

recommendations.title: Consolidate Presenter.UpdateUI callback into an event dispatcher
recommendations.detail: Multiple tabs call presenter.UpdateUI(func() { ... fyne.Do(...) }) to marshal UI updates back to the main thread. The pattern is functional but indirect — caller wraps presenter.UpdateUI which wraps fyne.Do. Over ~15+ callsites (rules_tab.go, add_rule_dialog.go, edit_dialog.go), this creates a chain. Propose: internal abstraction ui/configurator/event_dispatcher.go with Dispatch(callback) that routes presenter updates through a single entry point, making thread-safety guarantees visible and testable.

recommendations.title: Audit UI → core/ui imports for layering violations
recommendations.detail: core_dashboard_tab.go imports ui/configurator (line 31) and wizardmodels (line 32) — main tab reaches into the configurator package. While not a hard cycle, it creates coupling: Core tab can trigger wizard actions. Verify that this is intentional architectural decision (main dashboard should be able to launch configurator) vs accidental layering violation. If intentional, document the contract in ARCHITECTURE.md.

monoliths.problem: CreateClashAPITab is a 1300+ LOC single closure capturing 24+ variables (selectedProxyNames, hidePingErrors, sortNameAscending, selectionAnchorVis, applySavedSort, reconcileListSelection, refreshServersProxySelectionUI, applyServersPointerSelection, etc.) with nested state machines for sort/filter/selection, SRS button handling, ping concurrency, endpoint override. Logic spans sort functions (lines 730-819), selection handlers (lines 636-717), refresh callbacks (lines 603-634), ping-all workers (lines 822-915), and proxy list item generation (lines 427-562).
monoliths.decomposition: Extract into: (1) ui/clash_api_tab_state.go with ServersTabState{selectedProxies, sortType, hidePingErrors, selectionAnchor} + methods Reconcile(), ApplySortOrder(), ToggleFilter() — holds mutable state; (2) ui/clash_api_tab_handlers.go with OnLoadProxies(), OnPingProxy(), OnSwitchProxy(), OnSortBy() — closures bound to ServersTabState + controller, reducing variable capture; (3) keep CreateClashAPITab as thin orchestrator that builds UI widgets and binds them to handlers. Result: ~300 LOC per file, clear responsibility boundaries, testable state + handlers.

monoliths.problem: ShowAddRuleDialog spans 1146 LOC as a single-pass closure creating the dialog UI, registering field change listeners, syncing between form and raw JSON tabs, validating input (domain/IP/process/path patterns), and handling save. Complex state machines for tab switching (activeTabIsRaw toggle at line 88), process selection popup (processesSelected list + selectProcessesButton callback), and JSON ↔ form bidirectional sync (lines ~350-500) are nested within the closure.
monoliths.decomposition: Extract into: (1) ui/configurator/dialogs/rule_editor_state.go with RuleEditorState{label, domain, ips, urls, processes, pathPatterns, outbound, customJSON} + methods SyncFromJSON(), SyncToJSON(), Validate() — canonical state holder; (2) ui/configurator/dialogs/rule_editor_tabs.go with buildFormTab(state, presenter) and buildRawTab(state) functions — tab UI builders (each ~250 LOC); (3) ui/configurator/dialogs/rule_editor_process_picker.go with showProcessPickerDialog() — separates process selection logic; (4) keep ShowAddRuleDialog as thin wrapper that creates state, builds tabs, registers sync handlers, calls onSave. Result: ~300 LOC per file, state explicitly serializable/testable, tab sync logic isolated.

monoliths.problem: ShowEditDialog spans 1071 LOC as a single-pass closure handling: outbound tag/type/comment input fields, urltest-specific options (interval/tolerance/URL from template variable dropdowns), scope selector (global/per-source), body merging for referenced entries (lines 66-77), validation, and save callback. Interleaved logic: tag entry creation (lines 79-82), type select with conditional urltest options display (lines 85-134), comment input, scope selector with source filtering (lines 141-180+), body JSON display, save/delete buttons (lines ~800-900).
monoliths.decomposition: Extract into: (1) ui/configurator/outbounds_configurator/edit_state.go with OutboundEditState{tag, type, comment, interval, tolerance, url, scope, sourceIndex} + methods ApplyTemplate(merged), Validate(), ToConfig() — state holder; (2) ui/configurator/outbounds_configurator/edit_form_builder.go with buildBasicFields(state), buildUrltestFields(state), buildScopeSelector(state) — tab UI builders; (3) ui/configurator/outbounds_configurator/template_resolver.go with ResolveMergedBody(editPresenter, existing) — moves the complex ref+preset merge logic (lines 62-77) into a standalone function; (4) keep ShowEditDialog as orchestrator. Result: ~250 LOC per file, state testable, template merge logic auditable.

stateFlows: Core Dashboard: [binary not found] ←→ [binary found] ←→ [starting] ←→ [running] ←→ [stopping] ←→ [stopped]; state.json missing gates Restart button; dirty config HighImportance restart indicator

ClashAPI tab: [not tested] → [testing] → [disabled/error] OR [enabled] → [loading proxies] → [list ready] ↔ [sorting/filtering/pinging]; selection state (none/single/multi) tracked across list refresh
Subscription operation: [idle] → [starting] → [downloading] → [progress updates] → [parsing] → [success toast (auto-hide 20s)] OR [error toast]; final state reverts parser UI

Refactor into wintun_cleanup_windows_device.go (~250 LOC device enumeration), wintun_cleanup_windows_nla.go (~350 LOC both NLA phases). Extract lazydll bindings + GUID constants to shared module. No behavior change; improves testability (each concern can be unit-tested separately).

recommendations.title: Simplify profiler.go dispatch logic into smaller functions
recommendations.detail: dispatch() function (internal/traffic/profiler.go:329-377) combines rolling-buffer GC, map eviction (conn/dnsAccum/dnsByIP), session matching, subscriber fanout. Extract: appendToRollingBuffer(e) (lines 331-339), evictExpiredDNSByIP(e) (lines 350-357), dispatchToSession(e, active) (lines 367-369), fanoutToSubscribers(e, subs). Each <50 LOC, single concern. No behavior change; improves readability and enables independent testing.

recommendations.title: Unify device_info caching pattern via generic sync.Once helper
recommendations.detail: device_info_*.go (all platforms) each define deviceInfoOnce sync.Once + loadDeviceInfo(). Minor duplication of caching wrapper. Opportunity: extract deviceInfoCache[T any] type with Do(fn func() T) T pattern, used by all three. Risk is low (pure extraction); benefit is low (pattern is trivial). Do NOT consolidate source-specific logic (sw_vers, /etc/os-release, wmic); only the caching skeleton.

recommendations.title: Document profiler lock granularity and consider refactoring hot paths
recommendations.detail: TrafficProfiler.mu (internal/traffic/profiler.go) is held across slow map operations (eviction sweep in eventsFromPoller, connProcessMap access on every event). During high-traffic capture (1000s events/sec) this becomes a bottleneck. Consider: split into connProcessMapMu + dnsAccumMu + dnsByIPMu + rollingBufferMu with narrower critical sections. Or use atomic.Ptr<map> with periodic snapshots. Profile-guided refactoring recommended before claiming performance optimizations.

recommendations.title: Review SendCtrlBreak (platform_windows.go) for signal safety in shutdown paths
recommendations.detail: SendCtrlBreak (internal/platform/platform_windows.go:67-75) calls GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent and returns immediately. No graceful timeout handling — caller must wait separately. Document contract: SendCtrlBreak does NOT wait for process exit; caller should poll or use separate wait. Alternatively, add SendCtrlBreakWithTimeout(pid int, timeout time.Duration) for convenience at call sites.

recommendations.title: Consider extracting power context creation/cancellation into PowerContextManager type
recommendations.detail: power_*.go (all platforms) repeat: powerCtx/powerCtxCancel sync + context.WithCancel + mu.Lock patterns. No behavior change, but extracted type (PowerContextManager with Start/Stop/Get methods) would simplify implementations and reduce duplication. Low risk; moderate benefit for readability.

recommendations.title: Add tests for cross-platform profiler.eventMatchesSession behavior
recommendations.detail: eventMatchesSession (internal/traffic/profiler.go:383-391) accepts verified/inferred confidence but explicitly rejects unattributed. Document and test: empty target string means system-wide (any event). Add tests for process-name match, path mismatch cases.

stateFlows: power_darwin.go: IOKit CFRunLoop subscription → goPowerCallback (C callback) → (async) sleepCallbacks/resumeCallbacks dispatch + powerCtx cancel/recreate
power_linux.go: DBus signal loop → PrepareForSleep(true/false) → sleepingFlag.Store + context cancel/recreate + (sync) callback dispatch
power_windows.go: Win32 message loop (locked OS thread) → WM_POWERBROADCAST handlers → powerSleeping flag + context cancel/recreate + (async via time.AfterFunc) callback
profiler.go: Clash poller.Out + LogTailer.Out → runJoin select loop → eventsFromPoller/eventsFromLogLine → dispatch → rolling buffer + session.Append + subscribers fanout

packages.responsibility: Complete user and developer reference for singbox-launcher architecture, data flows, API, templating system, parser, and configuration. Split across 8 primary docs (ARCHITECTURE, DATA_FLOW, API, TEMPLATE_REFERENCE, WIZARD_STATE, ParserConfig, TRAFFIC_PROFILER, + ancillary BUILD/RELEASE guides) covering schema v6 and SPEC 053-067 lifecycle.

keyFiles.purpose: Project structure, package responsibilities, core/ui/internal layers, major subsystems (AppController, ConfigService, UIService, ProcessService, Configurator wizard, API), cross-package dependencies
Load/save/build/preset-toggle flows with mermaid-style ASCII diagrams, state transitions, resolver pipeline, preset binding lifecycle
Debug API reference (24 endpoints, auth, responses, curl cookbook), traffic profiler control, snapshot endpoint, settings read/write
wizard_template.json schema, presets (outbounds/dns/rules/vars), #if construct (SPEC 067), runtime globals (@runtime.*), default_value resolution, formatting style guide
state.json v6 schema (canonical), connections/sources, outbound Ref/Updates (SPEC 058), rules (preset/inline/srs kinds), DNS, vars, migrations from v5
Subscription parser: 9 protocols (VLESS/VMess/Trojan/SS/Hy2/SSH/SOCKS5/Naive/WireGuard), Xray JSON array, share URI encode/decode, filters, tag prefix/postfix/mask
User guide for Traffic Profiler window (per-process capture, live/domains/IPs/connections tabs, verbose toggle, issue classification)
AI-agent scope, task format (Spec Kit), restrictions, documentation checklist, where to find code/SPECS/patterns

recommendations.title: Update ARCHITECTURE.md — snapshot.go package underdocumented
recommendations.detail: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 (structure) omits core/snapshot/ and its Build() function used by /debug/snapshot endpoint and Copy snapshot UI button. Snapshot.go builds state+template+cache+config into one JSON for bug reports (mentioned in README.md and API.md but no inventory in ARCHITECTURE). Recommendation: add entry for core/snapshot/ under API section with purpose and Build() signature.

recommendations.title: ARCHITECTURE.md §Детальное описание (lines 582-643) references deleted files
recommendations.detail: Section claims `ui/wizard_overlay.go` contains ChildWindowsOverlay and FocusOpenChildWindows callback logic (line 611). File exists but structure differs: ChildWindowsOverlay is in ui/configurator/child_overlay.go, FocusOpenChildWindows is callback in UIService. Also claims ui/components/click_redirect.go exists for click-redirect but that file handles ChildWindowsOverlay focus only, not general click-redirect. Recommendation: cross-reference actual file locations in core/uiservice/ui_service.go for real callback registration points.

recommendations.title: DATA_FLOW.md — MigrateOutboundsToReferencedShape migration details incomplete
recommendations.detail: §1 Load flow correctly describes SPEC 058 one-shot migration but omits interaction with SPEC 060 v5→v6 schema conversion. On fresh v0.9.8+ no state.json exists, so MigrateOutboundsToReferencedShape never runs (path A / legacy v5 assumed pre-SPEC-058). Missing clarification: migration only applies to v5 state files loaded into v6 system; new installs skip it. Recommendation: add note that migration is legacy-only (v5→v6 scope).

recommendations.title: API.md — rebuild-config response shape mismatch documented
recommendations.detail: Line 151 claims doc-comment promises {"rebuilt":bool} but handler returns {"ok":true}. Documented as "на доработке" (pending fix). Correct handler behavior verified (ok only). Recommendation: either implement {"rebuilt":bool} response or remove the note and confirm {"ok":true} is canonical.

recommendations.title: TEMPLATE_REFERENCE.md §4.2 (preset DNS servers) — substitute vars scope mismatch
recommendations.detail: Line 92 claims preset.vars can be substituted in bundled dns_servers[].body on build. Actual code (core/build/preset_expand.go): preset vars ARE substituted in rule/dns_rule/rule_set fragments but DNS server bodies use per-database tag prefix, no deep preset-var substitute per entry. DNS servers inherit outer vars only if declared as part of preset definition. Recommendation: clarify that dns_servers[] inline server definitions can use preset vars but pooled template servers[tag] do not.

recommendations.title: TEMPLATE_REFERENCE.md §9.6 (outer @-only) — runtime globals restriction unclear
recommendations.detail: Line 460 states "Runtime globals в outer if[] запрещены" but line 455 shows params[].if DOES accept @runtime.platform in its predicates within inner #if contexts. Outer if[] accepts ONLY bool vars (@tun, @enable_proxy_in), but the doc doesn't clearly separate outer if[] from inner #if predicates. Real rule: outer params[].if[] is @bool-only; inner #if within config/param value CAN use @runtime.*. Recommendation: add subsection clarifying the three scopes: (1) outer if[]/if_or[] on params/vars/presets — @bool only; (2) inner #if predicates within config value — @var + @runtime.*; (3) preset-local vars — same rules as outer.

recommendations.title: ParserConfig.md — Xray JSON array handling incomplete for v0.9.8+
recommendations.detail: Lines 34-60 describe SPEC 033 Xray JSON array parsing with lengthy coverage of remarks→slug, jump detour chains. However, doc omits SPEC 067 interaction: if template has #if on outbounds[] with @runtime.platform predicates, generated outbounds may be filtered at build time, affecting Xray node visibility in selector filters. Recommendation: add cross-reference to SPEC 067 for conditional inbound/outbound visibility during Xray expand.

recommendations.title: Build a per-file responsibility matrix in ARCHITECTURE.md
recommendations.detail: Current §Структура provides hierarchical tree but lacks quick lookup table. Developers must parse nested structure to find e.g., which file handles rule validation, where share-URI encode lives, where preset expand happens. Recommendation: add 2-3 line per-file responsibility matrix after tree (file path | purpose | key function) as supplementary quick-ref.

recommendations.title: Add ARCHITECTURE.md §Package dependency diagram
recommendations.detail: No visual or explicit cycle/layering checks documented. CONSTITUTION.md defines no circular import rules, but complex cross-package callbacks (UIService ← Configurator business ← core/state ← core/build ← core/template) need explicit documentation. Recommendation: add directed graph (or ASCII DAG) showing core → services → ui layering, callbacks, and MCP/interface boundaries.

recommendations.title: WIZARD_STATE.md §3 (schemas) needs v6 version pinning note
recommendations.detail: Doc says state.json v6 is canonical and all future loads normalize v5→v6, but doesn't specify: when does Save force v6? After first Save of v5 state? Immediate on Load? Add note: all writes post-SPEC-060 are v6 (meta.version=6, schema=presets_v1), no dual-write mode. v5 files upgraded in-memory at Load.

recommendations.title: Create missing ARCHITECTURE addendum: ADR register
recommendations.detail: Current ARCHITECTURE.md is snapshot of code structure, not design decisions. SPEC/ folder contains ADRs (SPEC 046, 053, 057, 058, 060, 067) but ARCHITECTURE.md doesn't link to them or explain WHY major design choices (thin refs, preset binding, resolver pipeline, snapshot format). Recommendation: add optional §Architectural Decision Records with links to SPEC/ PLAN/SPEC.md for cross-references (e.g., why Ref+Updates model vs full body in state, why preset bundles not inline, why atomic write with Rename).

recommendations.title: Document all-go-files inventory (missing from ARCHITECTURE.md)
recommendations.detail: Tree shows 50+ files but no complete file manifest. Developers adding features must grep to find where e.g., process-matching rules are validated, where SRS auto-download lives (core/build/srs.go? not mentioned), where HWID generation happens (core/subscription? also not listed). Recommendation: either expand ARCHITECTURE.md with 1-line inventory of all .go files under core/, ui/, internal/, or create separate docs/FILE_INVENTORY.md.

recommendations.title: WIZARD_STATE.md — clarify preset.dns_server.local_tag namespace
recommendations.detail: §3.2 describes DNSServer.ref as "<pid>:<tag>" but doesn't explain: are local_tag values unique per preset or global? If preset A and B both declare dns_servers[tag="cloudflare"], do they collide in state? Docs imply per-preset uniqueness but actual code may enforce global uniqueness via build pipeline. Recommendation: clarify that preset-dns-server refs are scoped to preset ("russian:cloudflare" vs "ru-direct:cloudflare" are different entries).

recommendations.title: Missing: core/build/ and core/state/ inventory in ARCHITECTURE.md
recommendations.detail: These two packages are architectural hearts (Resolver pattern, lifecycle sync, migration logic) but given only cursory mention (1-2 lines each) in ARCHITECTURE.md. No mention of core/state/v6/ (State, Rule, DNSServer schema), core/state/load.go, core/state/save.go, core/build/resolve_*.go, core/build/sync_*.go. Recommendation: add subsection §Build & State packages with file-level inventory and lifecycle responsibilities.

deadOrHistorical.details: Line 286 claims (error_banner.go удалён в SPEC 068 — ошибки показываются через dialogs.go). This is accurate but error_banner.go was removed years ago (before v0.9.0). The comment documents a historical decision (why dialogs.go not error_banner.go), not current drift. **Action:** keep as historical note if educational; move to separate §Legacy notes section or delete if SPEC 068 is closed.

Section §config.updater.go marked as removed with comment "cache-refresh pipeline переехал в core/config_service.go". This is accurate (SPEC 052 refactored subscription fetch). The comment is correct but dated (describes v4→v5 schema change). **Action:** verify config_service.go::UpdateConfigFromSubscriptions still defines the documented pipeline (yes, confirmed); keep note but clarify it's a v5+ implementation detail.

Lines claim UIService manages UI state and callbacks (UpdateUI, callbacks for child windows, tray menu). Actual implementation: UIService stores Fyne pointers and callback functions, but window management (child overlay focus) is in ui/configurator/child_overlay.go, not UIService directly. The callback IS stored in UIService but registered in configurator.go. **Action:** clarify UIService holds callback references but does not implement focus logic itself; focus is handled by child_overlay.go.

Lines claim PATCH /state/dns/rules replaces USER rules, preserving preset-rules. Actual API implementation (core/debugapi/state_endpoints.go PATCH /state/dns/rules): replaces only user-kind entries, preset-kind entries remain. **Action:** code matches doc; no change needed but note that this is index-sensitive (preset entries must be at correct positions in array for preservation).

Lines 49-50 claim MigrateOutboundsToReferencedShape on Load converts empty Ref + tag-in-template to Ref="#TEMPLATE#" + diff. Correct for v5→v6 path, but misleading: this is a ONE-SHOT legacy migration (SPEC 058 scope was v5 pre-state; v6-native state.json never has empty Ref). **Action:** clarify migration is for v5 state files only; native v6 state always has explicit Ref or omitted (direct).

Line 73 claims 'ref' field is `{pid}:{tag}` format for preset dns-servers. Omits question: can users edit/delete preset DNS servers? Or are they read-only? Field is Enabled toggleable but text doesn't clarify ownership. **Action:** update to say preset DNS servers are template-owned with toggle enabled/disabled but no edit/delete UI visibility (handled by DNSTagLocked in UI layer).

POST /action/rebuild-config documented as returning {"rebuilt":bool} but actual handler returns {"ok":true}. Internal doc comment (code) claims it should return rebuilt flag. **Action:** either implement {"rebuilt":true} response or update doc to {"ok":true} only and note that caller should check /state to verify rebuild.

README claims 11 UI locales and self-update check at startup with notification. Verify these are accurate in current version. **Action:** verify internal/locale/ has 11 JSON files (en, ru, de, es, fr, it, ja, ko, pt-br, tr, zh) and core/core_version.go::CheckLauncherVersionOnStartup() still runs.

Lines describe StateService cache (GetCachedLauncherVersion, GetCachedVersion for core removed in SPEC 046). This is accurate but OLD removal (v0.8.x era). Comment should clarify: all version caches are now state.json and settings.json; GetCachedLauncherVersion is last artifact. **Action:** if core-version cache is gone, remove this; if launcher-version cache is kept, move comment inline to say why it exists.
